title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
description: Given an array nums that is sorted in ascending order and then rotated at some pivot, write a function to search for a target value target. If found in the array, return its index; otherwise, return -1. You must write an algorithm with O(log n) runtime complexity.

examples.explanation: The target 0 is located at index 4 in the rotated sorted array.
The target 3 is not present in the array, hence the output is -1.
The array contains only one element 1, and since 0 is not present, the result is -1.

constraints: The input array nums must be of size n where 1 <= n <= 10^5.
Each element in nums is an integer within the range [-10^4, 10^4].
hints: Consider how the array is divided into two sorted halves.
Determine which side of the array to explore based on the target value.
Check for edge cases when the array contains duplicates.
Try to identify the pivot point where the rotation occurs.
